science.title: Scientists just found a hidden weakness in forever chemicals
science.summary: Researchers discovered that hydrogen radicals generated by intense UV light can break down stubborn PFAS “forever chemicals” without added chemicals. The breakthrough reveals a key mechanism that could lead to greener and more effective technologies for permanently destroying these pollutants.

science.title: Giant underground neutrino detector brings scientists closer to cracking the neutrino puzzle
science.summary: Deep beneath the ground in China, the massive JUNO neutrino observatory has delivered its first major scientific breakthrough, achieving one of the most precise measurements yet of how elusive neutrinos change as they travel. Using just 59 days of data, researchers sharply improved measurements of key neutrino properti

science.title: Scientists Finally See How Antibodies Really Attack Viruses
science.summary: A new nanodisc breakthrough lets scientists see viruses more realistically, revealing hidden clues that could lead to better vaccines. Viruses are highly effective at infecting human cells, largely because of specialized proteins that cover their outer surfaces. These proteins are also a key focus for vaccine design. T

science.title: Stanford Scientists Discover Explosive New Type of Immune Cell
science.summary: Scientists studying the remarkable regenerative abilities of planarian flatworms have uncovered a previously unknown type of immune cell with an unusually destructive defense strategy. What if an immune cell could wipe out nearby threats by detonating itself? That is exactly what Stanford researchers have discovered in

science.title: Scientists discover parrots may actually use names
science.summary: Parrots may be doing more than just repeating words—they may actually use names. By analyzing hundreds of recordings from pet parrots, researchers found evidence that many birds use specific names to identify particular people, animals, and even individual companions. Some parrots appeared to refer to someone who wasn’

tech.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
tech.summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said Tuesday that the company is working on over 40 different AI wearable devices — including jewelry, earbuds with cameras, pins, and watches — a sign of how aggressively the chipmaker is betting that the next major computing platform won't be a phone.

tech.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
tech.summary: Smart glasses are still a nascent category, but chipmaker Qualcomm is hard at work upgrading the silicon to power the next wave of XR devices: the Snapdragon Reality Elite. Although Qualcomm is announcing the chip today at Augmented World Expo, we've technically already gotten a hands-on with a device powered by the ne

tech.title: Year of free HPE software a “step in the correct direction” in VMware rivalry
tech.summary: Partner tells Ars that HPE should be giving out more free VM Essentials licenses.

tech.title: Mobileye is entering the US robotaxi market with standalone service
tech.summary: The service will leverage its Moovit platform to launch in an a US city in 2027.

tech.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
tech.summary: Debt sale set to test investor appetite for further exposure to AI sector amid a deluge of borrowing.

medicine.title: Retinal photographs can help predict Alzheimer's disease risk factors
medicine.summary: Often called "the window to the soul," the eyes may also offer clues about something less poetic but just as important: the health of the brain. A new study of tens of thousands of patients has revealed that cheap, simple and common photographs of the retina at the back of the eye can accurately predict many of the mos

medicine.title: Developing clinical-grade intestinal organoids for regenerative medicine applications
medicine.summary: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and other intestinal disorders affect millions of people worldwide, often causing lasting damage to the lining of the gut. In severe cases, this damage can become irreversible, leaving surgery as one of the few remaining treatment options. In recent years, however, regenerative medicine

medicine.title: Tirzepatide outperformed semaglutide weight-loss drug results in real world patients, study shows
medicine.summary: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RAs) have become popular for weight loss, but results vary from person to person and from drug to drug. Venky Soundararajan and colleagues explored the full range of responses to tirzepatide (e.g., Mounjaro or Zepbound) and semaglutide (e.g., Ozempic, Wegovy or Rybelsus)

medicine.title: Online CBT eases stress after broken-heart syndrome, especially in women
medicine.summary: Men are overrepresented when it comes to heart disease, but there are certain conditions that mainly affect women, such as "broken-heart syndrome." Stress and anxiety are common in this group. A new study led by researchers at Uppsala University and Karolinska Institutet shows that internet-based cognitive behavioral t

medicine.title: Psychologists say patients are turning to chatbots as mental health professionals
medicine.summary: More than three-quarters of psychologists report their patients are discussing artificial intelligence in therapy, using the technology to seek additional support with their mental health, find a diagnosis, or for friendship and intimate relationships, according to a survey by the American Psychological Association.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics
mathematics.summary: Grothendieck is revered in the world of math; outside of it, he’s known for his unusual life, if he’s known at all. But what were his actual mathematical contributions? The post How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Explore JPL to Take Place Oct. 10, 11

space.title: Watch European rocket launch record-breaking mission for Amazon on June 17
space.summary: An Ariane 6 heavy-lift rocket will launch a record-breaking load to orbit early on Wednesday morning (June 17), and you can watch the action live.

space.title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites to orbit on 1st launch as a public company (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 24 Starlink satellites lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on Monday, June 15, 2026.

space.title: Live from the moon! How lasers connected us all to NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts on their epic lunar trip
space.summary: A laser system, previously tested on board the International Space Station, helped to stream video directly from the Artemis 2 mission on its way around the moon.

space.title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
space.summary: Liftoff of the Falcon 9 rocket occurred at 8:27 a.m. ET, just over an hour before SpaceX went public on the NASDAQ stock market.

astronomy.title: Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System?
astronomy.summary: A rare meteorite recovered from the Sahara Desert could be a fragment of a Moon-size body that met a violent end in the earliest days of the solar system. The post Did A Moon-Size Planet Grow Fast and Die Young in the Early Solar System? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21
astronomy.summary: The three planets in the western twilight are pulling away from each other now. On Wednesday, the Moon will occult Venus in daylight. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: The Little Red Dots That Turned Out to Be Black Holes in Disguise
astronomy.summary: For three years they've been one of the strangest puzzles in astronomy. Tiny, mysterious red dots scattered across the early universe, so abundant and so bright that some researchers wondered if they had "broken" cosmology itself. Now the James Webb Space Telescope has captured the most detailed look yet at one of them

astronomy.title: The Galaxy's Spin Is Hiding in the Hum of Gravitational Waves
astronomy.summary: We are used to thinking of gravitational waves as messengers from catastrophes in space, the ringing of spacetime after black holes collide for example. But our own Galaxy hums with a fainter, steadier signal, a chorus of millions of unseen binary stars. A new study has found that this hum carries a hidden fingerprint

astronomy.title: FAST Finds a Pulsar in an Almost Flawless Circular Orbit
astronomy.summary: Somewhere in the plane of the Milky Way, a dead star is spinning 220 times a second, and it's circling its companion in almost the most perfect orbit astronomers have ever measured. China's giant FAST radio telescope has just found it, and the shape of that orbit is a near flawless record of a billion year relationship

architecture.title: Designing for Stray Cities: Architecture Beyond the Human
architecture.summary: Architecture continues to draw cities as though humans occupy them alone. Plans trace circulation routes, zoning maps assign functions, and buildings are evaluated according to human comfort, safety, and efficiency. Walking through cities across India and Southwest Asia reveals something much more complex. Dogs sleep b

architecture.title: Breathe Lodge Residence / Jérôme Lapierre Architecte
architecture.summary: Nestled within the natural landscape of Quebec's Eastern Townships, Breathe Lodge Residence offers a thoughtful living experience designed to foster a meaningful relationship between dwelling and nature. Conceived as a family retreat removed from the pace of urban life, the residence integrates into its surroundings wi

architecture.title: Lego House / Fernanda Marques Arquitetos Associados
architecture.summary: Casa Lego unfolds as an encounter between nature, light, and architecture across its 1,390 m². Designed by architect Fernanda Marques, the project was born from a family’s desire to live in an urban, cosmopolitan São Paulo without giving up the feeling of being surrounded by greenery, light, and silen

architecture.title: Hilltop House / W O V E N Architecture and Design
architecture.summary: Perched atop a steep, rocky slope on a Gulf Island in British Columbia, the angular form of the Hilltop House hugs the contours of the land. Designed as a refuge for a Toronto-based client seeking respite from urban chaos, this home is more than a dwelling—it's a meditation on light, landscape, and longevity.

architecture.title: NBBJ wraps Vivo skyscraper in spiralling terrace
architecture.summary: Architecture studio NBBJ has completed the headquarters of Chinese phone company Vivo in Shenzhen, China, which includes a terrace wrapped around the exterior of the skyscraper. construction.title: Industry leaders recognised in King’s Birthday Honours
construction.summary: Figures from across the civil engineering, infrastructure, construction, water, energy and transport sectors have been recognised in the King’s Birthday Honours. The post Industry leaders recognised in King’s Birthday Honours appeared first on New Civil Engineer.
